info.name: VirusTotal API v3 - Threat Graphs
Threat Graphs
VirusTotal Search Graphs

params.name: filter
params.description: Return the graphs matching the given criteria only

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number graphs to retrieve

params.name: cursor
params.description: Continuation cursor

params.name: order
params.description: Sort order

params.name: attributes
params.description: Specific fields to retrieve

items.docs:
Endpoint used to search graphs.

There are a set of multiple modifiers that you can use to refine your search results. You can combine all of them together and use them in conjunction with AND, OR and NOT operators.

Date and numeric fields support the suffix plus or minus to match values greater or less than the passed value. If not sign has been added to the modifier, you will get exact matches. You can use more than once the same modifier in the same query to define ranges: `creation_date:201

items.docs: This endpoint returns the comments posted in a given graph. Check [Graph comments relationship page](https://gtidocs.virustotal.com/reference/graph-comments) to know more about the returned object.

info.name: VirusTotal Get Object Descriptors Related to a Graph

params.name: id
params.description: A 65 char length id which uniquely identify the graph.

params.name: relationship
params.description: Relationship name (see [table](ref:graph-object#relationships))

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of related objects to retrieve

params.name: cursor
params.description: Continuation cursor

items.docs: This endpoint is the same as [/graphs/{id}/{relationship}](https://gtidocs.virustotal.com/reference/graphs-relationships) except it returns just the related object's IDs (and context attributes, if any) instead of returning all attributes.

info.name: VirusTotal Get Objects Related to a Graph

params.name: id
params.description: A 65 char length id which uniquely identify the graph.

params.name: relationship
params.description: Relationship name (see [table](ref:graph-object#relationships))

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of related objects to retrieve

params.name: cursor
params.description: Continuation cursor

items.docs:
Graph objects have number of relationships to other objects. As mentioned in the  [Relationships](https://gtidocs.virustotal.com/reference/relationships) section, those related objects can be retrieved by sending `GET` requests to the relationship URL. 

Some relationships are accesible only to users who have access to Google Threat Intelligence.

The relationships supported by graph objects are documented in the [Graph](https://gtidocs.virustotal.com/reference/graph-object) API object page.

``

info.name: Threat Graphs Permissions & ACL
VirusTotal Get Users and Groups That Can Edit a Graph

params.name: id
params.description: A 65 char length id which uniquely identify the graph.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of related objects to retrieve

params.name: cursor
params.description: Continuation cursor

items.docs:
This endpoint returns the users and groups that can edit the graph. The graph needs to be shared with them as `Editor` to appear here.

```json Response example
{
    "data": [
        {
            "attributes": {
                "first_name": "Bertram",
                "last_name": "Gilfoyle",
                "profile_phrase": "",
                "reputation": 1,
                "status": "active",
                "user_since": 1530008602
            },
            "id": "gilfoyle",
